description: Ground truth labels for the vuln_demo benchmark. All findings are deliberately crafted CWE vulnerabilities. Criticality is assessed under benchmark_tight.yml constraints.

examples/vuln_demo/main.c:15:security.in.notes: Unbounded strcpy into 16-byte stack buffer; under 2KB stack constraint any overflow is fatal. Function copy_input is called from main with attacker-controlled argv.

examples/vuln_demo/main.c:22:core.NullDe.notes: Unconditional dereference of sensor_value which is NULL on the argc<=2 path. On a safety system (ASIL-B) a null deref in a sensor read is a hard fault with no recovery path.

examples/vuln_demo/main.c:35:unix.Malloc.notes: Memory leak: packet allocation not freed on the header-alloc-failure path. Under 4KB heap, repeated calls exhaust the allocator.

examples/vuln_demo/main.c:52:unix.Malloc.notes: Use-after-free: buf is freed then passed to printf. Detected by clang-sa unix.Malloc checker. UAF in embedded context causes unpredictable memory corruption.

examples/vuln_demo/main.c:58:alpha.core..notes: Integer overflow in rows*cols multiplication before malloc on a safety system (ASIL-B). Under ISO 26262, integer overflow violates numerical safety invariants.

examples/vuln_demo/main.c:69:core.uninit.notes: Uninitialized return value in compute_checksum when mode!=1. Low-severity under tight constraints since checksum is not safety-critical in this context.

examples/vuln_demo/main.c:76:security.in.notes: ISR function (isr_uart prefix) + critical_functions list + ASIL-B. Unbounded strcpy in an ISR on a hard-RT 50us system is maximum severity.

examples/vuln_demo/main.c:83:core.NullDe.notes: control_loop is in critical_functions; measured_ptr is NULL on argc<=4 path. Null deref in a PID/control loop on a safety system causes immediate loss of control.
